diagrams.title: Access Matrix Model
diagrams.description: A conceptual matrix showing subjects (users/domains) in rows and objects (files/resources) in columns, with access rights in each cell
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how access rights are represented systematically between subjects and protected objects

diagrams.title: Bell-LaPadula Security Levels
diagrams.description: Hierarchical security classification showing how information flows are restricted
elements: Arrow UP (read): Only upward (no read up)
Arrow DOWN (write): Only downward (no write down)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing confidentiality protection by preventing unauthorized information flow between security levels

diagrams.title: OS Security Architecture
diagrams.description: Layered architecture showing components of a trusted operating system
elements: System calls: Interface between user and kernel mode
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how security is implemented at different layers of the operating system

diagrams.title: User Mode vs Kernel Mode
diagrams.description: CPU execution modes showing privileged and unprivileged operations
elements: User Mode: Limited instructions, restricted memory access
Kernel Mode: All instructions, full hardware access
Protected resources: Memory, I/O devices, CPU states
System Call Interface: Controlled entry point to kernel
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hardware-level protection mechanisms and why separation is essential for security
